title: ranunculus rigescens f. leiocarpus
intro: Ranunculus rigescens f. leiocarpus, also known as the Lesser Celandine, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The Lesser Celandine is known to provide several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and reducing swelling and pain. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
